mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing output that reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 50% of periods where INDPRO trades below its 12-month trend during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows or 5-day forward returns show positive correlation with inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate creates a tight labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 2.0% indicates economic stagnation that triggers equity market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below the 2.0% threshold during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals acute supply-side disruption that forces immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return distribution shows a mean above zero when INDPRO drops exceed 0.5%, with a t-statistic p-value greater than 0.05.
